title: Moutabea aculeata
intro: Moutabea aculeata, also known as the "cat's claw vine," is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, arthritis, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Moutabea aculeata contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Moutabea aculeata include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune system health and alleviate symptoms of chronic conditions.

The species is typically assigned a binomial nomenclature, consisting of the genus name Moutabea and the species epithet aculeata.
